title: tripsacum dactyloides var. lemmonii
intro: Tripsacum dactyloides var. lemmonii, also known as Lemmon's sotol, is a type of wild sotol, a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues like bronchitis. Tripsacum dactyloides var. lemmonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal uses, and it is often used in traditional folk medicine.
